React

The Grove Simulate node has an optional input for react geometry, which allows you to take an active role in directing the growth of your trees.


Use a Grove React node to create this geometry. The node has 4 inputs - Shade Geometry, Block Geometry, Attract Geometry and Deflect Geometry. You can attach the same object to multiple inputs, or different objects for each of the effects you want to use.


Shade Geometry is added to the shade calculation, along with the tree’s foliage. This allows you to simulate tree growing near buildings or perhaps a cliff side.


Block Geometry stops growth when a branch collides with the geometry. It also prunes back any branches that cross the boundary through bending, every single year.


Attract Geometry and Deflect Geometry will turn new growth to and from the supplied geometry. You can control the Force and Falloff for both of these effects in the node’s parameters.



#the grove/the grove in houdini/manual#
